destinations.evidence: Receipt construction detects scheduled transactions via as_scheduled and fills origin_tx, call_id, index, initial_block, frequency, expiry/max_block, ttl, caller, gas, fees, and value.
Otterscan receipt shape includes scheduled fields but leaves them unset when transaction context is unavailable.
destinations.snippet: origin_tx: None, call_id: None, index: None, initial_block: None, frequency: None, max_block: None, ttl: None, caller: None, gas: None, max_fee_per_gas: None, max_priority_fee_per_gas: None

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port: scheduled receipt metadata is populated from the scheduled transaction when transaction context is present; generic paths leave scheduled fields unset.
rationale: The current receipt builder maps TxType::Scheduled to ReceiptEnvelope::Scheduled and extracts scheduled data through as_scheduled. The local Otterscan path exposes the same shape but leaves scheduled fields None because it lacks the transaction context.
statement: RPC receipt construction for scheduled transactions must use the Scheduled receipt envelope and populate scheduled fields from the transaction via as_scheduled, including origin_tx, call_id, index, initial_block, frequency, max_block/expiry, ttl, caller, gas, fee, and value metadata; non-scheduled receipts leave scheduled fields unset.
suggested_falsification_tests: Build a receipt for a scheduled transaction and assert all scheduled extension fields are populated; build a legacy receipt and assert they remain None.
summary: Scheduled RPC receipts expose scheduled metadata.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Scheduled receipt conversion should expose scheduled metadata from TransactionSigned::as_scheduled.
title: Scheduled RPC receipts expose scheduled metadata
